description_long: This is a robust, angular guard stone constructed primarily from weathered stone, featuring a triangular base with cut-out sections and a slanted, diamond-shaped upright slab. The upright slab displays a circular black-and-white photographic plaque or medallion set into its upper portion, and beneath it, there are engraved inscriptions in a foreign script, likely commemorative or informational. The stone shows signs of aging, with visible discoloration, surface erosion, and some moss or lichen growth, especially along the edges and base. The base is broad and stable, designed to prevent vehicles from mounting the curb or to mark a boundary. The object is set on a paved surface, surrounded by concrete tiles and some scattered dry leaves. Its overall appearance suggests it serves both a protective and commemorative function in an urban environment.
description: A weathered stone guard stone with a triangular base, diamond-shaped upright slab, and a circular plaque with inscriptions, set on a paved surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the engraved inscriptions and circular plaque prominently on the upright slab, with the triangular base and surrounding pavement visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth of the triangular base and the side profile of the upright slab, with the plaque and inscriptions partially visible.
description_view_2: Side view focusing on the thickness of the upright slab and the geometric cut-outs in the base, with minimal visibility of the plaque or inscriptions.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back of the upright slab, the triangular base, and some weathering, with the plaque only partially visible.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban sidewalks, near intersections, driveways, or historical sites, often adjacent to pedestrian pathways or at the entrance to restricted areas.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, with the base resting flush on a paved or concrete surface and the upright slab angled for visibility.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface discoloration, chipping along the edges, and some smoothing on the base, likely caused by weather exposure, occasional vehicle contact, and foot traffic. Moss or lichen growth is present in crevices and along the lower edges.
attribute_historical_markings: Engraved inscriptions in a foreign script and a circular photographic plaque or medallion set into the upper portion of the upright slab.
